hero.title: ZIAN AI CONCEPTS
hero.lede: I build modern web and app products with AI at their core, run hands-on courses, and integrate generative AI into companies.

about.title: One person. One clear promise.
about.lede: ZIAN AI CONCEPTS is André Zimmermann — engineer, trainer, AI practitioner. No agency machinery, just a direct partner focused on measurable outcomes.

milestones.label: Engineer
milestones.title: Web & App Engineering
milestones.body: Deep experience in modern frontend (React, TypeScript) and backend integration. Clean code, fast builds, long-lived architecture.

milestones.label: AI Practitioner
milestones.title: AI in production
milestones.body: From RAG to agents to bespoke workflows — I build AI that actually solves problems.

milestones.label: Trainer
milestones.title: Courses that stick
milestones.body: Workshops and courses for teams that want to master AI, not hype it. From prompt engineering to coding with agents.

milestones.label: Solo Studio
milestones.title: Direct line
milestones.body: You talk to the person who also builds. Short paths, clear ownership, honest advice.

process.title: How I work.
process.lede: Clear steps, no magic. Switch the lens depending on your project.

steps.label: Discovery
steps.title: Understand, don't assume
steps.body: We align on goal, users, risks and success metric before a single line of code.

steps.label: Architecture
steps.title: Solid structure
steps.body: Lean tech choices, clear modules, sane dependencies — every choice justified, nothing trendy.

steps.label: Build
steps.title: Visible fast, built right
steps.body: Iterative, short cycles with working builds — feedback over waterfall.

steps.label: Train
steps.title: Hand over knowledge
steps.body: Documentation, walk-throughs and optional mini-workshops for your team.

steps.label: Handover
steps.title: Clean handover
steps.body: Repo, deploy, runbook and an optional maintenance retainer if you want it.

steps.label: Listen
steps.title: Listen before shipping
steps.body: Interviews with the people who'll actually use the AI — not just leadership.

steps.label: Use-Case
steps.title: Realistic use cases
steps.body: We prioritize by impact × feasibility, not by buzz.

steps.label: Prototype
steps.title: Fast proof
steps.body: A working prototype on your real data within 2-4 weeks.

steps.label: Integrate
steps.title: Into existing tools
steps.body: We integrate AI into your workflows — not another tool island.

steps.label: Train
steps.title: Enable the team
steps.body: Workshops + guardrails so your team can evolve the tooling on their own.

showcase.title: What I build for you.
showcase.lede: Three core offerings. One consistent quality promise.

services.title: AI Web & App Development
services.body: Idea to product: landing pages, SaaS, mobile apps — all with AI features where it makes sense.

services.title: AI Courses & Training
services.body: Hands-on workshops for developers, teams or leadership — from prompting to productive AI workflows.

services.title: Enterprise AI Integration
services.body: I guide the adoption of AI into existing tools, processes and teams — strategically and technically.

cases.title: AI-first SaaS dashboard
cases.body: Real-time insights with an embedded copilot. Cuts onboarding time by 60%.

cases.title: Agent-based support
cases.body: Multi-agent system that resolves tier-1 tickets autonomously and escalates cleanly.

cases.title: Mobile AI coach app
cases.body: Personalized coaching with on-device inference and cloud fallback.

aiDemo.title: Talk to a tiny model.
aiDemo.lede: Get a feel for what AI inside a product is like. Try a suggestion or ask your own question.
aiDemo.greeting: Hi! I'm the demo bot for ZIAN AI CONCEPTS. Ask me about pricing, courses, or how AI integration actually works.
aiDemo.error: Whoops — the endpoint isn't reachable right now.
suggestions: How much does a project cost?
How do I roll AI into an SMB?
How long does a prototype take?

replies.hello: Hey! Glad you're here. Ask away — I'll answer with concrete examples.
replies.pricing: Pricing depends on scope. Web projects usually start with a 1-2 week discovery sprint. Drop me a line about your project and I'll come back with a concrete quote within 24 hours.
replies.courses: Courses come as in-house workshops (1-3 days) or remote sessions. Content is tailored to your team — from prompt engineering to agent architectures.
replies.integration: We start with use-case discovery (which workflows really benefit?), prototype quickly on your data, and integrate into existing tools instead of building yet another island.
replies.timeline: A prototype is typically visible in 2-4 weeks. Full production roll-outs usually take 6-12 weeks depending on integrations and data readiness.
replies.fallback: Great question! In a real setup the answer depends on the model + your context. This is a demo — drop me a line directly and I'll answer in person.

contact.title: Let's talk.
contact.lede: Tell me briefly what you're up to — I'll get back to you within 24 hours.

form.name: Name
form.error: Something went wrong. Please reach out via email instead.
